exercises.title: Conversion: String to Number
exercises.instructions: Given s = '42', convert it to a number, add 8, and print the result.
exercises.hints: Number(s) converts a numeric string to a number

exercises.title: Conversion: Number to String
exercises.instructions: Given age = 15, print 'Age: 15' by converting age to a string and concatenating.
exercises.hints: String(age) converts the number to text

exercises.title: Type Inspector
exercises.instructions:
Create four variables:
- a number called age (value: 16)
- a number called height (value: 1.75)
- a string called city (value: "Auckland")
- a boolean called isStudent (value: true)

Print the type of each variable (using typeof) on a separate line.
exercises.starter_code:
// Create your variables here


// Print each type with typeof
exercises.hints: Use typeof: console.log(typeof age). JavaScript has one number type for ints and decimals.

exercises.title: Conversion: Decimal to Integer
exercises.instructions: Given price = 9.8, convert to an integer (truncate toward zero) and print it.

exercises.title: Operator: Integer Division
exercises.instructions: Given a = 17 and b = 5, print the integer (floor) division of a by b.
exercises.hints: JavaScript has no // operator — use Math.floor(a / b)

exercises.title: Operator: Modulo
exercises.instructions: Given a = 17 and b = 5, print the remainder of a divided by b.

exercises.title: Temperature Converter
exercises.instructions:
Given celsius = 25, convert it to Fahrenheit using the formula: F = (C * 9/5) + 32.
Print the result as: 25°C is 77.0°F
exercises.hints: Use a template literal and .toFixed(1) to show one decimal place.

exercises.title: String Formatting: Template Literal
exercises.instructions: Given name = 'Mia' and score = 95, print 'Mia scored 95' using a template literal.
exercises.hints: Inside a template literal (backticks), put variables inside ${ }

exercises.title: Multiple Assignment
exercises.instructions: Assign a, b, c = 1, 2, 3 in a single line using array destructuring, then print their sum.
exercises.starter_code:
let [a, b, c] = [?, ?, ?];
console.log(a + b + c);
exercises.hints: JavaScript unpacks the array [1, 2, 3] into a, b, c via destructuring

exercises.title: Swap Two Variables
exercises.instructions: Given a = 1 and b = 2, swap them using array destructuring and print 'a=2 b=1'.
exercises.hints: Write [a, b] = [b, a] — swaps without a temp variable

exercises.title: Augmented Assignment
exercises.instructions: Start with total = 10 and add 5 using +=, then print total.
exercises.hints: += adds the right-hand side in place
